basics.name: Corbet Griffith
basics.label: UX Engineer
basics.summary: UX professional bridging the gap between rigorous user research, visual design, and technical deployment. Over 10 years of experience building user-focused digital solutions for brands like Ford and VML. Passionate about translating human-centered research, journey mapping, and usability testing directly into functional, optimized web experiences and intuitive UI architectures.

work.name: TerrAscend
highlights: Led comprehensive user journey mapping sessions to optimize IT support services, successfully reducing misrouted tickets by 80%.
Analyzed internal support requests to understand user pain points, leading to the implementation of a ZenDesk knowledge-base solution for employee self-servicing.
Developed step-by-step Standard Operating Procedures and digital signage to streamline user issue resolution and improve the overall employee experience.

work.name: Jackson Dawson
highlights: Conducted A/B tests to understand which web content best drives user conversions, implementing a new registration flow that increased lead submissions by 15%.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to map processes, create user stories, and define user acceptance testing criteria for agile sprint deployments.

work.name: VML
highlights: Facilitated journey mapping sessions in Figma with strategists to understand user needs and partner pain points within the GuestXP Dealer Management System.
Partnered with subject matter experts to implement personalized digital experiences utilizing Adobe Experience Cloud and Analytics for Target.

work.name: Garden State Flower Cooperative
highlights: Gathered requirements through participatory design sessions and journey mapping to identify user problems in the existing pre-order process.
Wrote functional requirements to build a new app and pre-order process, directly addressing user pain points for buyers, farmers, and administrators.
